---
title: "Drought Risk Analysis"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Kodu gĂ¶rmek iĂ§in tÄ±klayÄ±n"
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---


## 1. Introduction & Data Overview

Climate change is no longer a future projection but a present reality for Turkey. Positioned in the Mediterranean basin, the country faces unique vulnerabilities. This Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) aims to quantify the environmental shifts and their correlation with agricultural output.

The Dataset
The analysis integrates three primary data streams:

Thermal Data (T2M): Average temperature at 2 meters above the earth's surface (Â°C).

Hydrological Data (PRECTOTCORR): Corrected precipitation amounts (mm/day).

Agricultural Value: The monetary value of crop production (Thousand TL) at the provincial level, aggregated into regional totals.

The data was wrangled to ensure temporal consistency and spatial alignment, transforming raw provincial Excel files and regional CSVs into a "Tidy Data" format for robust visualization.


2. Hydrological Shifts: Precipitation Volatility
While temperature shows a steady climb, precipitation is characterized by its unpredictability. For an agrarian economy, the distribution of water is as critical as the total amount.
```{r}
#| label: turkiye-bolgeler-sicaklik
#| message: false

#| label: turkiye-tum-bolgeler-yagis
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(tidyverse)


marmara_pr <- read_csv("data/marmara_precipitaion2000-2025.csv", skip = 9) %>% mutate(Region = "Marmara")
ic_anadolu_pr <- read_csv("data/iĂ§anadolu_precipitaion.csv", skip = 9) %>% mutate(Region = "Ä°Ă§ Anadolu")
akdeniz_pr <- read_csv("data/akdeniz_precipitation.csv", skip = 9) %>% mutate(Region = "Akdeniz")
dogu_anadolu_pr <- read_csv("data/doguanadolu_precipitation.csv", skip = 9) %>% mutate(Region = "DoÄźu Anadolu")
ege_pr <- read_csv("data/egebĂ¶lgesi_precipitaion_average2000-2025.csv", skip = 9) %>% mutate(Region = "Ege")
guneydogu_anadolu_pr <- read_csv("data/guneydoguanadolu_precipitation.csv", skip = 9) %>% mutate(Region = "GĂĽneydoÄźu Anadolu")
karadeniz_pr <- read_csv("data/karadeniz_precipitation.csv", skip = 9) %>% mutate(Region = "Karadeniz")


turkiye_precip_all <- bind_rows(
  marmara_pr, ic_anadolu_pr, akdeniz_pr, dogu_anadolu_pr,
  ege_pr, guneydogu_anadolu_pr, karadeniz_pr
)


turkiye_bolge_yagis <- turkiye_precip_all %>%
  group_by(YEAR, Region) %>%
  summarise(Bolge_Ortalama_Yagis = mean(ANN, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")


ggplot(turkiye_bolge_yagis, aes(x = YEAR, y = Bolge_Ortalama_Yagis, fill = Region)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", alpha = 0.7) + 
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "darkblue", se = FALSE, linetype = "solid", size = 0.8) + 
  facet_wrap(~ Region, ncol = 3) + 
  labs(
    title = "TĂĽrkiye'nin 7 BĂ¶lgesinde YÄ±llÄ±k Ortalama YaÄźÄ±Ĺź MiktarÄ± (2000-2025)",
    subtitle = "Renkli sĂĽtunlar yÄ±llÄ±k ortalamayÄ±, koyu mavi Ă§izgi ise genel yaÄźÄ±Ĺź eÄźilimini gĂ¶sterir",
    x = "YÄ±l",
    y = "Ortalama YaÄźÄ±Ĺź (mm/gĂĽn)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none", 
    strip.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )
```
Analysis of Water Stress
The Black Sea region remains the outlier with high and relatively stable precipitation. However, the Aegean and Central Anatolia regionsâ€”Turkey's primary grain and fruit basketsâ€”show concerning levels of volatility. The downward or stagnant trend lines in these regions, combined with rising temperatures, indicate a high risk of "evapotranspiration," where plants lose moisture faster than the soil can replenish it.


Next stage of our EDA explores how the economic value of crops has changed alongside these climatic shifts.
```{r}


#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Veri YĂĽkleme ve BirleĹźtirme AdÄ±mlarÄ±"
#| label: turkiye-7-bolge-sicaklik
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(tidyverse)

# Fonksiyon: DosyayÄ± oku, ilk 9 satÄ±rÄ± atla ve bĂ¶lge ismi ekle
read_region <- function(file_path, region_name) {
  read_csv(file_path, skip = 9) %>%
    mutate(Region = region_name)
}

# TĂĽm sÄ±caklÄ±k dosyalarÄ±nÄ± oku ve birleĹźtir
turkiye_temp <- bind_rows(
  read_region("data/akdeniz_T2M.csv", "Akdeniz"),
  read_region("data/doguanadolu_T2M.csv", "DoÄźu Anadolu"),
  read_region("data/ege_bĂ¶lgesi_temperatureat2metersrange_2000-2025.csv", "Ege"),
  read_region("data/guneydoguanadolu_tT2M.csv", "GĂĽneydoÄźu Anadolu"),
  read_region("data/iĂ§anadolu_T2M.csv", "Ä°Ă§ Anadolu"),
  read_region("data/karadeniz_T2M.csv", "Karadeniz"),
  read_region("data/marmara_T2M_2000-2025.csv", "Marmara")
)

# BĂ¶lge bazlÄ± yÄ±llÄ±k sÄ±caklÄ±k Ă¶zeti
turkiye_bolge_ozet <- turkiye_temp %>%
  group_by(YEAR, Region) %>%
  summarise(Bolge_Ortalama_Sicaklik = mean(ANN, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# Grafik: 7 BĂ¶lge Yan Yana
ggplot(turkiye_bolge_ozet, aes(x = YEAR, y = Bolge_Ortalama_Sicaklik, color = Region)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) + # 'size' yerine 'linewidth' gĂĽncel sĂĽrĂĽmler iĂ§in daha iyidir
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "black", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ Region, ncol = 3) + 
  labs(
    title = "TĂĽrkiye'nin 7 BĂ¶lgesinde YÄ±llÄ±k Ortalama SÄ±caklÄ±k DeÄźiĹźimi (2000-2025)",
    x = "YÄ±l",
    y = "SÄ±caklÄ±k (Â°C)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none", 
    strip.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1) 
  ) # <-- Eksik olan parantez burasÄ±ydÄ±, eklendi.
```
```{r}



library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

# Excel dosyasÄ±ndan sadece verilerin olduÄźu C5:D31 aralÄ±ÄźÄ±nÄ± okuyoruz.
# col_names ile sĂĽtunlara kendimiz isim veriyoruz ki iĹźimiz kolaylaĹźsÄ±n.
veri <- read_excel("data/uretim_turkiye.xlsx", 
                   range = "C5:D31", 
                   col_names = c("Yil", "Uretim_Degeri"))

# Excel'de metin olarak algÄ±lanan yÄ±llarÄ± ve ĂĽretim deÄźerlerini sayÄ±ya Ă§eviriyoruz
veri$Yil <- as.numeric(veri$Yil)
veri$Uretim_Degeri <- as.numeric(veri$Uretim_Degeri)

# ggplot2 ile zaman serisi Ă§izgi grafiÄźini oluĹźturuyoruz
ggplot(veri, aes(x = Yil, y = Uretim_Degeri)) +
  geom_line(color = "#2c3e50", linewidth = 1.2) +    
  geom_point(color = "#e74c3c", size = 2.5) +        
  labs(
    title = "TĂĽrkiye Bitkisel Ăśretim DeÄźeri (1995 - 2021)",
    subtitle = "YÄ±llara GĂ¶re Ăśretim DeÄźiĹźimi",
    x = "YÄ±l",
    y = "Bitkisel Ăśretim DeÄźeri (Bin TL)"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(veri$Yil), max(veri$Yil), by = 2)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma_format(big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ",")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1) 
  )
```


The nominal value of agricultural production has grown exponentially, especially in the Mediterranean and Aegean regions. While this appears positive, it must be viewed with caution. This rise is likely influenced by inflation and the shift toward high-value, export-oriented greenhouse crops. The divergence between "rising value" and "deteriorating climate conditions" suggests that Turkish agriculture is becoming increasingly capital-intensive, requiring more investment in irrigation and fertilizers to combat climate stress.
```{r}

# Gerekli kĂĽtĂĽphaneler
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

# 1. Ăśretim verisini okuma
veri <- read_excel("data/uretim_turkiye.xlsx", 
                   range = "C5:D31", 
                   col_names = c("Yil", "Uretim_Degeri"))

veri$Yil <- as.numeric(veri$Yil)
veri$Uretim_Degeri <- as.numeric(veri$Uretim_Degeri)

# 2. Enflasyon verisini okuma 
# DÄ°KKAT 1: read_csv YERÄ°NE read_excel KULLANIYORUZ!
# DÄ°KKAT 2: read_excel isimsiz sĂĽtunlara ...1, ...2, ...3 isimlerini verir.
tufe_raw <- suppressMessages(read_excel("data/enflasyon.xlsx", 
                                        col_names = FALSE, 
                                        n_max = 24))

# Ä°lk sĂĽtunu YÄ±l olan satÄ±rlarÄ± alÄ±p aylarÄ± (...2 ile ...13 arasÄ±) seĂ§iyoruz
tufe_df <- tufe_raw %>%
  mutate(Yil = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(...1))) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Yil)) %>%
  select(Yil, ...2:...13) %>%
  distinct(Yil, .keep_all = TRUE) # Ă‡ift okuma ihtimaline karĹźÄ± sadece gerĂ§ek endeksi tut

# AylÄ±k endeks deÄźerlerini sayÄ±sal tipe Ă§evirme
tufe_df[, 2:13] <- lapply(tufe_df[, 2:13], function(x) suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x)))

# YÄ±llÄ±k ortalama TĂśFE endeksini hesaplÄ±yoruz (12 ayÄ±n ortalamasÄ±)
tufe_df$Yillik_Tufe <- rowMeans(tufe_df[, 2:13], na.rm = TRUE)

# Sadece YÄ±l ve YÄ±llÄ±k Ortalama TĂśFE'yi ayÄ±rÄ±yoruz
tufe_secili <- tufe_df %>% select(Yil, Yillik_Tufe)

# 3. Verileri BirleĹźtirme ve Reel DeÄźer Hesaplama
# Reel DeÄźer = (Nominal DeÄźer / YÄ±llÄ±k TĂśFE Endeksi) * 100
birlesik_veri <- inner_join(veri, tufe_secili, by = "Yil") %>%
  mutate(Reel_Uretim_Degeri = (Uretim_Degeri / Yillik_Tufe) * 100)

# 4. Enflasyondan ArÄ±ndÄ±rÄ±lmÄ±Ĺź GrafiÄźi Ă‡izdirme
ggplot(birlesik_veri, aes(x = Yil, y = Reel_Uretim_Degeri)) +
  geom_line(color = "#2c3e50", linewidth = 1.2) +    
  geom_point(color = "#e74c3c", size = 2.5) +        
  labs(
    title = "TĂĽrkiye Bitkisel Ăśretim DeÄźeri (Enflasyondan ArÄ±ndÄ±rÄ±lmÄ±Ĺź)",
    subtitle = "YÄ±llara GĂ¶re Reel Ăśretim DeÄźiĹźimi (2003 = 100 BazlÄ± Fiyatlarla)",
    x = "YÄ±l",
    y = "Reel Bitkisel Ăśretim DeÄźeri (Bin TL)"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(birlesik_veri$Yil), max(birlesik_veri$Yil), by = 2)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma_format(big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ",")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1) 
  )
```



```{r}


library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(scales)
library(ggplot2)

# --- 1. YAÄžIĹž VERÄ°SÄ°NÄ°N HAZIRLANMASI ---
# (Mevcut CSV dosyalarÄ±nÄ±n data klasĂ¶rĂĽnde olduÄźu varsayÄ±lmaktadÄ±r)
marmara_pr <- read_csv("data/marmara_precipitaion2000-2025.csv", skip = 9) %>% mutate(Region = "Marmara")
ic_anadolu_pr <- read_csv("data/iĂ§anadolu_precipitaion.csv", skip = 9) %>% mutate(Region = "Ä°Ă§ Anadolu")
akdeniz_pr <- read_csv("data/akdeniz_precipitation.csv", skip = 9) %>% mutate(Region = "Akdeniz")
dogu_anadolu_pr <- read_csv("data/doguanadolu_precipitation.csv", skip = 9) %>% mutate(Region = "DoÄźu Anadolu")

turkiye_precip_all <- bind_rows(marmara_pr, ic_anadolu_pr, akdeniz_pr, dogu_anadolu_pr)

# YÄ±llÄ±k ortalama yaÄźÄ±ĹźÄ± hesaplÄ±yoruz
yagis_verisi <- turkiye_precip_all %>%
  group_by(YEAR) %>%
  summarise(Ortalama_Yagis = mean(ANN, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  rename(Yil = YEAR) # BirleĹźtirme kolaylÄ±ÄźÄ± iĂ§in sĂĽtun adÄ±nÄ± "Yil" yapÄ±yoruz

# --- 2. ĂśRETÄ°M VERÄ°SÄ°NÄ°N HAZIRLANMASI ---
# (uretim_turkiye.xlsx dosyasÄ±nÄ±n data klasĂ¶rĂĽnde olduÄźu varsayÄ±lmaktadÄ±r)
uretim_verisi <- read_excel("data/uretim_turkiye.xlsx", 
                            range = "C5:D31", 
                            col_names = c("Yil", "Uretim_Degeri"))

uretim_verisi$Yil <- as.numeric(uretim_verisi$Yil)
uretim_verisi$Uretim_Degeri <- as.numeric(uretim_verisi$Uretim_Degeri)

# --- 3. VERÄ°LERÄ° BÄ°RLEĹžTÄ°RME (Sadece ortak yÄ±llar) ---
# inner_join sadece her iki veri setinde de olan yÄ±llarÄ± korur (Ă–rn: 2000-2021 arasÄ±)
birlesik_veri <- inner_join(yagis_verisi, uretim_verisi, by = "Yil")

# --- 4. KORELASYON GRAFÄ°ÄžÄ° (Scatter Plot) ---
# Korelasyon katsayÄ±sÄ±nÄ± hesaplayÄ±p grafiÄźe ekleyebiliriz (isteÄźe baÄźlÄ± ama gĂĽzel durur)
korelasyon <- cor(birlesik_veri$Ortalama_Yagis, birlesik_veri$Uretim_Degeri, use = "complete.obs")

ggplot(birlesik_veri, aes(x = Ortalama_Yagis, y = Uretim_Degeri)) +
  geom_point(color = "#e74c3c", size = 3) + 
  # Trend Ă§izgisi ekliyoruz (doÄźrusal iliĹźkiyi gĂ¶rmek iĂ§in)
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "#2c3e50", se = TRUE, fill = "lightblue", alpha = 0.5) + 
  labs(
    title = "TĂĽrkiye: YaÄźÄ±Ĺź MiktarÄ± ile Bitkisel Ăśretim DeÄźeri Ä°liĹźkisi",
    subtitle = paste("Sadece Ortak YÄ±llar (", min(birlesik_veri$Yil), " - ", max(birlesik_veri$Yil), ") | Korelasyon (r): ", round(korelasyon, 2), sep=""),
    x = "Ortalama YaÄźÄ±Ĺź (mm/gĂĽn)",
    y = "Bitkisel Ăśretim DeÄźeri (Bin TL)"
  ) +
  # Ăśretim deÄźeri bĂĽyĂĽk sayÄ±lar olduÄźu iĂ§in okunabilirliÄźi artÄ±rÄ±yoruz
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma_format(big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ",")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14)
  )
```

```{r}




library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(scales)

# --- 1. BÄ°TKÄ°SEL ĂśRETÄ°M VERÄ°SÄ° ---
# Veriyi okuyup yÄ±llar ve deÄźerleri sayÄ±sal formata Ă§eviriyoruz
uretim_veri <- read_excel("data/uretim_turkiye.xlsx", 
                          range = "C5:D31", 
                          col_names = c("Yil", "Uretim_Degeri")) %>%
  mutate(
    Yil = as.numeric(Yil),
    Uretim_Degeri = as.numeric(Uretim_Degeri)
  )

# --- 2. TARIM ALANLARI VERÄ°SÄ° ---
# Veriyi okuyup temizliyoruz
alan_veri <- read_excel("data/tarimurunualanlar.xlsx", skip = 3)
colnames(alan_veri) <- c("Ana_Kategori", "Alan_Turu", "Yil", "Alan")

temiz_alan_veri <- alan_veri %>%
  fill(Alan_Turu, .direction = "down") %>% 
  filter(!is.na(Yil)) %>%
  mutate(
    Yil = as.numeric(Yil),
    Alan = as.numeric(Alan)
  ) %>%
  # Ă–NEMLÄ°: Ăśretim verisi TĂĽrkiye geneli toplam olduÄźu iĂ§in,
  # o yÄ±la ait tĂĽm farklÄ± alan tĂĽrlerini (meyve, sebze, tahÄ±l vb.) topluyoruz.
  group_by(Yil) %>%
  summarise(Toplam_Alan = sum(Alan, na.rm = TRUE))

# --- 3. VERÄ°LERÄ° BÄ°RLEĹžTÄ°RME VE ORANLAMA ---
# Sadece iki dosyada da olan (ortak) yÄ±llarÄ± birleĹźtiriyoruz
birlesik_veri <- inner_join(uretim_veri, temiz_alan_veri, by = "Yil") %>%
  mutate(
    # Ä°steÄźin doÄźrultusunda: Bitkisel Ăśretim / TarÄ±m AlanÄ±
    Birim_Alan_Degeri = Uretim_Degeri / Toplam_Alan
  )

# --- 4. GĂ–RSELLEĹžTÄ°RME ---
ggplot(birlesik_veri, aes(x = Yil, y = Birim_Alan_Degeri)) +
  geom_line(color = "#8e44ad", linewidth = 1.2) + # Ă‡izgi rengi (Mor tonlarÄ±nda)
  geom_point(color = "#2c3e50", size = 3) +       # Nokta rengi
  # Genel trendi gĂ¶rmek iĂ§in istersen aĹźaÄźÄ±daki satÄ±rÄ±n baĹźÄ±ndaki '#' iĹźaretini kaldÄ±rabilirsin
  # geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "gray50", linetype = "dashed", se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "TĂĽrkiye: Birim TarÄ±m AlanÄ± BaĹźÄ±na Ăśretim DeÄźeri",
    subtitle = "Hesaplama: Toplam Bitkisel Ăśretim DeÄźeri / Toplam TarÄ±m AlanÄ±",
    x = "YÄ±l",
    y = "Birim Alan DeÄźeri (Bin TL / Dekar)"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(birlesik_veri$Yil), max(birlesik_veri$Yil), by = 2)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma_format(big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ",")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )
```
```{r}



#| label: bolgesel-tarim-analizi
#| message: false
#| warning: false


library(readxl)   # Excel okumak iĂ§in tek Ă§Ă¶zĂĽm budur
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)

yol <- "data/uretim_il.xlsx"
df <- read_excel(yol, col_names = FALSE)


sehirler <- as.character(df[2, 4:ncol(df)])

# Veri 3. satÄ±rdan baĹźlÄ±yor. 3. sĂĽtun YIL, 4. sĂĽtun ve sonrasÄ± ĹžEHÄ°R DEÄžERLERÄ°
ham_veri <- df[3:nrow(df), 3:ncol(df)]
colnames(ham_veri) <- c("Yil", sehirler)

# 4. TEMÄ°ZLE VE UZUN FORMATA Ă‡EVÄ°R
uzun_veri <- ham_veri %>%
  mutate(Yil = as.numeric(Yil)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Yil)) %>% # YÄ±l olmayan satÄ±rlarÄ± at
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Yil, 
    names_to = "Il", 
    values_to = "Uretim_Degeri"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Uretim_Degeri = as.numeric(Uretim_Degeri),
    # "Adana-1" gibi isimleri sadece "Adana" yapÄ±yoruz
    Il = trimws(gsub("-[0-9]+", "", Il)) 
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Uretim_Degeri))
# 5. BĂ–LGE EĹžLEĹžTÄ°RMESÄ° (SayÄ±lar eĹźitlendi: Toplam 83 satÄ±r)
bolge_haritasi <- data.frame(
  Il = c(
    # Marmara (11 isim)
    "Edirne", "KÄ±rklareli", "TekirdaÄź", "Ä°stanbul", "Kocaeli", "Yalova", "Sakarya", "Bilecik", "Bursa", "BalÄ±kesir", "Ă‡anakkale",
    # Ege (9 isim)
    "Ä°zmir", "Manisa", "AydÄ±n", "Denizli", "MuÄźla", "Afyonkarahisar", "Afyon", "KĂĽtahya", "UĹźak",
    # Akdeniz (9 isim)
    "Antalya", "Burdur", "Isparta", "Mersin", "Ä°Ă§el", "Adana", "Hatay", "Osmaniye", "KahramanmaraĹź",
    # Ä°Ă§ Anadolu (13 isim)
    "Ankara", "Konya", "Karaman", "EskiĹźehir", "Kayseri", "NevĹźehir", "Yozgat", "Sivas", "KÄ±rÄ±kkale", "KÄ±rĹźehir", "NiÄźde", "Aksaray", "Ă‡ankÄ±rÄ±",
    # Karadeniz (18 isim)
    "Amasya", "Artvin", "BartÄ±n", "Bayburt", "Bolu", "Ă‡orum", "DĂĽzce", "Giresun", "GĂĽmĂĽĹźhane", "KarabĂĽk", "Kastamonu", "Ordu", "Rize", "Samsun", "Sinop", "Tokat", "Trabzon", "Zonguldak",
    # DoÄźu Anadolu (14 isim)
    "AÄźrÄ±", "Ardahan", "BingĂ¶l", "Bitlis", "ElazÄ±Äź", "Erzincan", "Erzurum", "Hakkari", "IÄźdÄ±r", "Kars", "Malatya", "MuĹź", "Tunceli", "Van",
    # GĂĽneydoÄźu Anadolu (9 isim)
    "AdÄ±yaman", "Batman", "DiyarbakÄ±r", "Gaziantep", "Kilis", "Mardin", "Siirt", "ĹžanlÄ±urfa", "ĹžÄ±rnak"
  ),
  Bolge = c(
    rep("Marmara", 11), 
    rep("Ege", 9),            # 10'dan 9'a dĂĽĹźĂĽrdĂĽk
    rep("Akdeniz", 9), 
    rep("Ä°Ă§ Anadolu", 13), 
    rep("Karadeniz", 18), 
    rep("DoÄźu Anadolu", 14), 
    rep("GĂĽneydoÄźu Anadolu", 9)
  )
)
bolge_ozet <- uzun_veri %>%
  left_join(bolge_haritasi, by = "Il") %>%
  filter(!is.na(Bolge)) %>%
  group_by(Yil, Bolge) %>%
  summarise(Toplam_Uretim = sum(Uretim_Degeri, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# 7. GĂ–RSELLEĹžTÄ°RME (Yan yana 7 BĂ¶lge GrafiÄźi)
ggplot(bolge_ozet, aes(x = Yil, y = Toplam_Uretim, fill = Bolge)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ Bolge, ncol = 3, scales = "free_y") + # Her bĂ¶lgenin grafiÄźini ayÄ±r
  labs(
    title = "TĂĽrkiye'nin BĂ¶lgelerine GĂ¶re Bitkisel Ăśretim DeÄźeri (2000-2025)",
    subtitle = "DeÄźerler Bin TL cinsindendir",
    x = "YÄ±l",
    y = "Toplam Ăśretim (Bin TL)"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_number(big.mark = ".", decimal.mark = ",")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    strip.text = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )
```
